title: Wash Dishes
summary: Wash dishes by hand or load a dishwasher correctly for clean results.
learnerPromise: You will be able to wash dishes by hand or load a dishwasher so that everything comes out clean and ready to use again.
whyItMatters: Clean dishes are essential for safe food preparation. Washing them properly removes food residue and bacteria that can cause illness or attract pests.
realLifeUses: Cleaning up after breakfast before leaving for the day
Washing pots and pans after cooking dinner
Loading the dishwasher efficiently after a family meal
Washing a water bottle or lunch container for reuse
result: Clean, dry dishes ready to be put away.
youWillLearn: Scrape off leftover food before washing
Wash in the right order (glasses first, greasy items last)
Rinse thoroughly and dry or air-dry dishes
Load a dishwasher so water reaches all surfaces
miniChallenge: Wash all the dishes from one meal. If you have a dishwasher, load it properly. If washing by hand, wash, rinse, and dry each item.
steps: Scrape leftover food into the trash or compost bin.
Fill the sink with warm soapy water, or set up a washing station.
Wash in order: glasses and cups, then utensils, then plates, then pots and pans last.
Rinse each item under warm running water to remove all soap.
Place items in a drying rack or dry with a clean towel.
completionCriteria: All dishes are free of food residue and soap
Dishes are rinsed clean and either dried or in a drying rack
The sink area is wiped down after washing
commonProblems: Washing greasy pans first and making the water too oily for other dishes
Not rinsing well, which leaves soap residue on dishes
Letting dishes pile up, which makes the job harder
tips: Wash dishes right after eating. Dried food is much harder to remove.
Soak pots and pans with stubborn residue for a few minutes before scrubbing.
Change the wash water if it gets cold or greasy.
